title: Children Prop
description: Learn how to pass elements as children to create flexible, composable components.

slides.title: What is `children`? 👶
slides.content:
# The Special `children` Prop

Every component receives a `children` prop. It represents whatever is placed between the opening and closing tags of the component.

## Example

```jsx
function Card(props) {
  return <View style={styles.card}>{props.children}</View>;
}

// Usage
<Card>
  <Text>This is inside the card</Text>
  <Text>So is this</Text>
</Card>
```

The two `<Text>` components become the `children` of `Card`. This allows you to build wrapper components that don't need to know their content in advance.

slides.title: Using Children
slides.content: A common pattern is a container with a title:

quizData.question: What does the `children` prop contain?

options.text: The elements nested inside the component

quizData.explanation: `children` is a special prop that holds the content between the opening and closing tags.

slides.title: Children Can Be Anything 🎁
slides.content: The `children` prop can be a single element, an array of elements, strings, numbers, or even `undefined`. You can also manipulate children (e.g., add props to them) using `React.Children` utilities, but that's more advanced.

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: Use the `children` prop to create flexible layout components like `Card`, `Container`, `Modal`, etc. This is the essence of composition in React.
